name: Chunky Mustard Beans with Red Peppers
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Julienne strips of red peppers (sweet)
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the stem end from the beans; cut the beans into 1-inch lengths. There should be about 8 cups. In a large pot of rapidly boiling water, cook the beans in batches until tender-crisp, about 3 minutes.
2. Transfer each batch to a large bowl of cold water to chill.
3. Blanch the peppers and onions similarly: boil peppers for 1 minute and onions for 2 minutes. Drain thoroughly.
4. In a medium bowl, blend together turmeric, mustard, flour, and salt with sugar until smooth.
5. Stir in cold water to make a smooth paste.
6. In a large heavy preserving kettle, combine vinegar and celery seeds and mustard seeds; bring to boil.
7. Stir in about 1 cup of the spiced vinegar into the mustard paste, then whisk the mustard paste into the spiced vinegar in the saucepan until smooth.
8. Cook over medium heat, whisking constantly until the sauce is smooth. Reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 5 minutes.
9. Pour in the drained vegetables; mix well and return to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer 4-5 minutes or until the vegetables are tender.
10. Ladle into hot sterilized jars, leaving 1/8 inch headspace. Seal.
11. Process these in a boiling water bath for 10 minutes for half-pint jars and 15 minutes for pint jars. Makes about 9 cups.
